label: Iran Killed
source: Al Jazeera / HRANA / Hengaw / Iran Health Ministry
contestNote: Iran Health Ministry (official floor): 1,444+ killed, 18,551+ injured. HRANA NGO: 2,800+ incl. 181+ children. Hengaw: 4,300+ total in first 10 days. Iran UN ambassador: ~10,000 civilian sites hit. Pentagon denies targeting civilian infrastructure. 3.2M Iranians displaced per UNHCR. Internet blackout (~1% connectivity / 305+ hrs) severely limits verification. Ali Larijani confirmed killed Mar 17–18. Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly wounded, family killed (unverified).

label: US Killed
deltaNote: 13 KIA confirmed: 6 Army Reserve killed in Kuwait drone strike (Feb 28), Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington (died Mar 8 from wounds), 6 KC-135 crew (names released by Pentagon — crash Mar 13 western Iraq, cause under investigation). ~140 wounded total. USS Gerald Ford fire (Mar 18) — no sailor deaths confirmed. Joe Kent (NCTC) resigned Day 19.

label: Israel Killed
source: Israeli health authorities / Haaretz / Times of Israel
deltaNote: 17+ killed in Israel total. Mar 18: Iranian cluster missile kills elderly couple (70s) in Ramat Gan — first confirmed cluster munition fatalities. Savidor Central Station damaged; shrapnel sites across Tel Aviv. IRGC stated barrage was 'in revenge for killing of Larijani.' 2,000+ Israelis wounded total.

label: Gulf Region Killed
deltaNote: Mar 17: Abu Dhabi missile killed 1 Palestinian civilian. Dubai airport fuel tank drone strike (no direct casualties). UAE Shah Gas Field attacked Mar 18 (no direct casualties confirmed). Kuwait US Embassy closed (Rubio order). Saudi Arabia intercepted 37 drones. 16+ vessels struck in Persian Gulf total. Qatar intercepted Iranian missile; blasts heard in Doha Mar 17.

label: Lebanon Killed
source: Lebanese health authorities / Lebanon Information Ministry
deltaNote: 850 killed including 107 children and 66 women; 32 health workers killed. 2,100+ injured. 800,000+ displaced (Day 19: ~700,000). IDF issued Tyre evacuation warning (Mar 18). IDF struck Bachoura neighborhood, central Beirut. 11 Lebanese Civil Defense members wounded in Bekaa Valley raid. Separate Israel-Lebanon ceasefire track forming (France involved; Hezbollah has not responded).

label: Brent Crude
deltaNote: Brent surged back to ~$108–110/bbl after Israel struck South Pars gas field (world's largest natural gas reserve) on Day 20 — the first attack on Iran's upstream gas infrastructure. Brief Day 19 decline reversed. Iran has attacked all six GCC states. 21 confirmed attacks on merchant ships in Gulf/Hormuz. Gulf Arab oil production down 10M bbl/day from pre-war levels.

label: Flights Cancelled
deltaNote: ~500+ tankers stranded in Persian Gulf. Hormuz selective blockade: Iran permitting China/India-flagged vessels. IMO emergency council meeting convened March 18 — UN maritime body seeking humanitarian corridor. Iran FM: Iran will permanently redesign Hormuz navigation rules even after war. 21 confirmed tanker attacks in Gulf/Hormuz total.

label: Countries Affected
deltaNote: Iran, Israel, Lebanon, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Oman, Iraq, Jordan, Azerbaijan, Turkey, Cyprus, France. Day 20: Iran attacked all six GCC states simultaneously — first time in history. South Pars gas field struck (shared with Qatar). B-1 bombers flying over Iranian airspace. USS Tripoli ARG with 31st MEU heading to theater. Intel Minister Khatib killed. Iran executed Israeli spy Keyvani.
